question: How many plants did I initially plant for tomatoes and chili peppers?
ground_truth_answer: The information provided is not enough. You mentioned planting 5 plants for tomatoes but you did not mention chili peppers.

retrieval_results.context:
User planted five tomato plants, now harvesting almost daily, enjoying the flavorful homegrown tomatoes in salads, sandwiches, and cooking, noting that homegrown tomatoes are much more flavorful than store-bought.
User excited to use garden-sourced fresh tomatoes for pasta sauce and seeks advice on maintaining healthy, productive tomato plants throughout the season.
User is planning weekly meals and seeks recipes that feature cucumbers as the main ingredient, and currently has three cucumber plants in their garden producing a plentiful harvest
User is looking for recipe ideas that feature fresh tomatoes as the main ingredient.
User is interested in making homemade pasta sauce from scratch with fresh tomatoes and is seeking guidance on how to do so.
User noticed yellowing and wilting leaves on tomato plants, identified overwatering as the cause, reduced watering frequency, and observed improved plant health.
User intends to cook Bruschetta and Tomato & Mozzarella Salad using fresh tomatoes and is seeking tips on storing fresh tomatoes to keep them fresh longer.
User is looking for a simple dill pickle recipe to preserve cucumbers
User wants advice on how to store cucumbers to keep them fresh for a longer period
User is excited to try the dill pickle recipe, harvests cucumbers daily, and seeks guidance on adjusting the recipe for smaller batches (a few jars at a time)
